page: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. The system features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. The server does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 125,034.15 and a PassMark CPU score of 87,484.14. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, reaching a top-tier read-only large block speed of 338.62 GB/sec and a strong write speed of 133.14 GB/sec. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Cryptographic capabilities are strong, as evidenced by a top-tier PassMark CPU encryption score of 111,858.14, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets.

Given the high memory density and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this server is configured for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, database operations, and large-scale data processing. Redis performance is strong, delivering 111.87 million SET operations per second. While the server lacks GPU acceleration, it handles medium-sized language model inference at a strong prompt processing speed of 203.12 tokens per second, though text generation speeds remain average. The bare-metal configuration offers high resource density and direct hardware access, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring massive memory footprints without the overhead of a hypervisor.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores and 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating at 3.5 GHz, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, reaching 338.62 GB/sec for large block reads. The processor configuration includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced vector extensions. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong database operations and Redis throughput, alongside top-tier cryptographic execution enabled by hardware-accelerated instruction sets. Single-core performance is average, making the server best suited for highly parallelized, memory-intensive workloads. It represents a qualitatively cost-efficient option for enterprise databases, in-memory analytics, and large-scale data processing requiring direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs and 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for enterprise database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs and 1024 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring high-density DDR4 RAM and top-tier multi-core processing for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 1024.0 GB DDR4 memory providing a high-density 1:8 RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory read bandwidth.
Dedicated bare-metal Intel Xeon 8375C processors operating at 3.5 GHz.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators.
Strong performance for database operations, Redis caching, and cryptographic tasks.
